question_text: A seesaw has a child of weight 400 N sitting 1.5 m from the pivot on the left side. Another child of weight 600 N sits on the right side. An adult pushes down with 200 N at 2 m to the right of the pivot. Where should the 600 N child sit to balance the seesaw?
explanation: Calculate total clockwise moment: (400 × 1.5) = 600 Nm. Calculate anticlockwise moment from adult: 200 × 2 = 400 Nm. The 600 N child must provide: 600 - 400 = 200 Nm anticlockwise. Distance = 200 ÷ 600 = 0.33 m. Actually: clockwise = 600 + (200 × 2) = 1000 Nm; anticlockwise = 600 × d; d = 1000 ÷ 600 = 1.67 m. Recalculating: (400 × 1.5) + (200 × 2) = 600 + 400 = 1000 Nm clockwise; 600 × d = 1000; d = 1.67 m. The child should sit 1 m from the pivot.
